history.feature: Harden Week 1: Fix Everything (code health audit)
Prune Week 1: Code Cleanup (DRY drawer + dead fns + inline styles)
Prune Week 1: Delight Polish (train bob + tighter sound + station/rainbow random)
Prune Week 1: Expert Panel + Validation (cycle 1 close-out review, 8.3/10)
Cycle 2 Build Day 1: Train Name Labels (🏷️)

notes:
Cycle 2 Build Week, Day 1 (Day 44, May 3): 🏷️ TRAIN NAME LABELS shipped. Wrote roadmaps/cycle-2-build.md (5-day plan: Day 1 Names, Day 2 Big Grid 16x10, Day 3 Cargo Missions, Day 4 Track Replay/Ghost Train, Day 5 Sound Packs). Implemented Day 1: trains gain optional `name` field (default ''); new Settings entry '🏷️ Name Your Trains' opens a modal listing each placed train with a colored swatch + 14-char ALL-CAPS text input; Save persists names via existing JSON serialization (auto-save + save slots inherit it for free); during play a small dark-pill label floats above the loco with rounded corners + light border, kept upright (sibling DOM, not child of rotated trainEl), tunnel-fades with the loco via opacity match, and respects reduced-motion (no bob). Live-rename works: editing names while trains are playing refreshes the labels without restart. Esc closes modal, Enter saves, outside-click closes. Live-tested at https://mikedyan.github.io/train-tracks/?v=44: random gen → named red train 'MARK' via modal → Play → 'MARK' pill rendered above loco at correct coords (rect 474x273, 50x20px, opacity 1, dark bg) → Stop → zero leftover labels in DOM → Reload page → 'MARK' persisted on red train. Zero console errors on deployed site. Code health: 10,144 → 10,335 lines (+191 net for new modal HTML + CSS + ~80 LOC JS). KNOWN LIMITATION: share-link byte-encoding does NOT carry names (would bloat the hash); names live in localStorage auto-save + save slots only. New patterns logged: LESSON-DAY44-A (normalize-helper for optional state fields built in many literal sites), LESSON-DAY44-B (floating sibling DOM > child-of-rotated-sprite for upright labels). Tomorrow Day 45 = Cycle 2 Build Day 2 (Bigger Grid Option — 16×10 toggle in Settings, must clip-or-pad existing tracks gracefully, disable puzzles in big-grid mode). Old notes preserved below.

Prior: Prune Week 1, Day 5: EXPERT PANEL + VALIDATION complete. Closed out Cycle 1 with a full 10-dimension re-score vs Day 14 baseline. Live-tested deployment at mikedyan.github.io/train-tracks: tutorial auto-shows on fresh visit (cleared localStorage → 'Drag a Track Piece!' overlay popped), random gen produced a 16-cell oval loop with 4 tunnel openings + auto-placed red train + scenery + 3 waiting-passenger markers, ▶️ Play → train animated cleanly with chuff bob → 📯 HONK button appeared as expected → ⏹️ Stop → no console errors. Toolbar inventory verified: top row ~13 buttons + round secondary cluster of 6 — vs 21 visible at start of week. Score deltas vs Day 14 (actual 5.3 / potential 7.2): First Impression 3→9, Clarity 7→9, Core Loop 7→9, Difficulty Curve 5→8, Juice 8→9, Replayability 4→7, Uniqueness 6→7, Bug-Free 1→9 (biggest delta), Visual 8→9, Addictiveness 4→7. Overall 8.3/10, +3.0 absolute, +1.1 vs Day 14 potential. Wishlist hit-rate from Day 14 panel: 7/10 shipped (HONK, animal reactions, weather, rainbow track, passenger delivery, progression unlocks, screenshot/share — only Doppler, bigger grid, deeper 'story' deferred to cycle 2). Code-health honest accounting: file 10,089→10,144 lines (+55 net, MISSED ≤9,900 prune target). UX win, code neutral. Recommendation for Cycle 2 prune week: hard rule end-of-prune file size ≤ start-of-prune. Open bugs: 0. JS parses. Review written to reviews/prune-cycle-1-review.md. Cycle 1 closed. 🚂✨ Tomorrow Day 44 = BUILD Week 2 Day 1 (Cycle 2 roadmap + first feature). Old notes preserved below.

Prior: Prune Week 1, Day 4: DELIGHT POLISH complete. Four small but meaningful improvements landed. (1) Train animation smoothness: added a sub-pixel vertical chuff bob to the locomotive (±1.2px sine of performance.now()*0.012) inside renderTrainAtProgress, plus per-car bob with index-based phase offset (±1.0px) so trailing cars wave gently behind the loco. Both honor prefers-reduced-motion. The bob is small enough to feel like life rather than wobble — chassis 'breathes' as it rolls. (2) Sound timing: tightened the play-start audio stack — startChugLoop delay 500→350ms and startSmokeLoop 300→200ms so the whistle blends into the chuff/smoke instead of opening a quiet gap. (3) Better default random generation: every random track now gets exactly one station promoted from a random straight (kid reward = 'Toot toot!' callout + passenger pickup if enabled), and 25% of tracks additionally promote one straight to a rainbow tile for sparkle/magic. Verified live: 10/10 random gens produced 1 station each, 1/10 produced a rainbow (within noise of 25%). Tunnels still appear at the original ~20% rate. (4) PRUNE balance: collapsed 4× 'snap to end-of-cell, render, crash, return' repetition in advanceTrainAnim into a single crashTrainHere(anim) helper. Browser regression check passed: random track → play → train chuffed through 5+ cells in 4s without crashing or visual stutter. JS parses cleanly. File 10137→10144 lines (+7 net; mostly inline comments + the new promote helper for station/rainbow). Tomorrow Day 43 = PRUNE Day 5 (Expert Panel + Validation — score 10 dimensions vs Day 14 baseline, write reviews/prune-cycle-1-review.md, close out cycle 1 prune week). Old notes preserved below.

Prior: Prune Week 1, Day 3: CODE CLEANUP complete. Three pruning wins this Wednesday: (1) DRY mobile drawer — #sidebar is now the single source of truth for palette items; #drawer-content is empty in HTML and populated at boot by new `buildMobileDrawer()` which clones sidebar children (h2→drawer-section-label span, palette-piece nodes verbatim). Removed ~37 lines of duplicated palette markup. (2) Killed 5 dead functions: `getCurrentBiome` (trivial getter never called), `isConnectedAt` (defined alongside `neighborConnected` but never used), `updateAllConnectionDots` (no callers), `startLongPress` (orphaned — `cancelLongPress` retained as defensive code), `cyclePaletteTrainColor` (only invoked from itself via setPaletteTrainColor). (3) Consolidated 11 toolbar inline-style button declarations into reusable CSS utility classes: `.btn-random`, `.btn-mute`, `.btn-save-modal`, `.btn-puzzle-modal`, `.btn-weather`, `.btn-biome`, `.btn-share`, `.btn-help`, `.btn-settings`, `.btn-passengers-icon`, `.btn-unlock-all` + `.volume-slider`. Final tally: 10,169 → 10,137 lines (-32 net), 351,975 → 347,935 bytes (-4,040 / -1.1%). 26 sidebar pieces + 26 drawer pieces verified live; share menu (2 items) + settings menu (5 items) confirmed working; toolbar button colors confirmed via getComputedStyle (rgb 156,39,176 etc.). JS parses cleanly. Tomorrow Day 42 = PRUNE Day 4 (Delight Polish) — train animation smoothness, sound timing, better default random gen, magical first 30 seconds. Old notes preserved.

Prior: Prune Week 1, Day 2: SIMPLIFY complete. Toolbar trimmed 21→~13 visible buttons by introducing two consolidating menus: ⚙️ Settings (groups Music, High Contrast, Stats, Keyboard Shortcuts, Fullscreen) and 📤 Share (groups Copy Link + Save Image). Cuts: removed standalone 🎵 music button + music-volume slider (folded into Settings, music-volume now lives in Settings panel), ⌨️ shortcuts button, 🔗 share-link button, 📸 screenshot button, 📊 stats button, ♿ high-contrast button, ⛶ fullscreen button. Fixed the ☀️ vs ☀️ icon collision: weather button now uses 🌤️ (sun behind small cloud) for sunny so it's distinct from the day/night ☀️/🌙 toggle. Updated `WEATHER_ICONS.sunny` constant accordingly. Made `toggleMusic()` DOM-button-optional (no longer requires #btn-music element); `restoreMusicSettings()` now updates the Settings menu's text label instead of styling a now-removed button. `toggleHighContrast()` and `onFullscreenChange()` similarly stripped of dead btn-id references. Esc key now closes the new Settings + Share modals. JS parses cleanly (`node --check` passed). Added shared CSS for `#shortcuts-overlay,#settings-overlay,#share-overlay` and `.settings-item` grid styling. File size: 10,089 → 10,169 lines (+80; modal HTML/CSS additions). Net negative is deferred to Day 41 (Wednesday code prune): mobile drawer DRY, dead-fn hunt, inline-style move — expected -170 lines, ending Prune week well below 9,900 target. Old Day-39 notes preserved below.

Prior: Prune Week 1, Day 1: Fresh Eyes Audit complete. Wrote PRUNE_REPORT.md (9.5KB) with 5-year-old usability walk-through, full inventory (21 toolbar buttons + 26 palette items + 270 JS functions), and Tuesday cut plan. Headline finding: toolbar has metastasized — second row is 10 cryptic single-emoji buttons (☀️ used twice for different actions, ⌨️ keyboard-shortcuts is a kid antipattern, ⛶ fullscreen / ♿ high-contrast / 📊 stats / 🎵 music are power-user clutter). Plan for Day 40 (Simplify): consolidate 🔊+🎵 into one sound toggle, fix ☀️ collision (🌗 day/night vs 🌦️ weather), move ⌨️/📊/♿/⛶ behind a single ⚙️ Settings menu, merge 🔗+📸 into a 📤 Share menu. Target end-state: 21 toolbar buttons → ~12-13. Code-side prune deferred to Day 41 (Wednesday): DRY mobile-drawer palette dupe (~50 lines), hunt dead functions (~100 lines), move inline styles to classes (~20 lines). Target file size after Prune week: ≤9,900 lines (down from 10,089 = net negative code, pass criterion). Sacred / non-cuts: HONK 📯, all 9 scenery items, all 5 train colors, all 9 track types, all 10 puzzles. Friday will re-score 10 dimensions vs Day-14 baseline. Old notes preserved below.

Prior: Harden Week 1, Day 5: Regression Pass — final ship-readiness check. Tested live deployment against original Day-1 promise (build, play, save, share). All 13 regression checks passed. File size unchanged at 10,089 lines (zero growth this Harden week). Harden Week 1 final tally: 1 bug found total (BUG-014 favicon, fixed same-day on Day 34), zero open. — final ship-readiness check. Tested live deployment against original Day-1 promise (build, play, save, share). All 13 regression checks passed: page load (zero train-tracks console messages), saved-track auto-restore, random generator (37 cells with auto-placed train), puzzle/sandbox round-trip, play/stop toggle, speed slider, save modal (3 slots), puzzle modal (10 cards, First Loop loads), share encoding (138-char hash, byte-identical roundtrip), palette completeness (all 26 piece types ×2 surfaces = 52 elements), all 9 track types + 5 trains + 3 cars + 9 scenery present, tutorial/stats/unlocks flags intact. Train-animation logic verified correct via direct function-stepping (60 cells advanced cleanly with crashing=false/finished=false); the `requestAnimationFrame` pause observed in real-time sampling was a CDP-tab artifact, not a code regression — confirmed by manual rendering working perfectly. File size unchanged at 10,089 lines (zero growth this Harden week). Harden Week 1 final tally: 1 bug found total (BUG-014 favicon, fixed same-day on Day 34), 1 bug fixed, zero open. Tomorrow: Day 39 = PRUNE Week 1 Day 1 (Fresh Eyes Audit).
